steps.title: Find the sentence boundary
steps.content: The first idea says plastic-bag consumption decreased by up to ninety percent. The next idea begins a new sentence explaining that such taxes are subject to the rebound effect. These are two complete clauses that need a full stop between them.

steps.title: Avoid comma and run-on errors
steps.content: Choice A joins two complete sentences with only a comma. Choice B uses "and" in a way that blurs the boundary after the percentage. Choice D creates an ungrammatical run-on with no punctuation.

steps.title: Separate the two ideas
steps.content: A period after "percent" correctly ends the first sentence and lets "Such taxes" begin the next one. The answer is C.
